title: Post-UMS regression: C++ Complex_fold_expression infinite loop (1 true regression, 2 pre-existing)
description:
Investigation results: **True UMS Regression (1 test):** 1. **C++ Complex_fold_expression** — Was passing (0.00s) pre-ums, now times out at 10s. Root cause: error recovery infinite loop. **Root Cause Analysis (Complex_fold_expression):** Input: `template void negateValues(Args... args) { ((std::cout << !args << " "), ...); }` Debug trace shows an infinite cycle starting at op ~20: - v0 (pos=58, state=7360, cost=0): main parse, stuck at pos=58 - v1 (pos=58, state=2056, cost=110): error recovery, stuck at pos=58 - v2 (pos=53, state=3772, cost=110): error recovery, stuck at pos=53 Because findActiveVersion picks lowest position, v2 (pos=53) always gets selected. v2 repeatedly spawns error recovery child (state=982, same position), which gets killed, and v2 loops. v0/v1 at pos=58 never advance. The C reference parser handles this fine by using PreferRight soft preferences to eventually swap/kill the lagging error recovery version. Our decisive-kills-only approach cannot kill v2 because all 3 versions have comparable costs (0 vs 110, amplification insufficient). **Not Regressions (2 tests):** - HTML Void_tags: Was already failing pre-ums (text instead of start_tag for
) - Java method_references: Was already failing pre-ums (scoped_type_identifier instead of field_access) **Fix:** Requires implementing soft preference handling (PreferLeft/PreferRight) in condenseStack, which was deliberately deferred in the UMS work.
